messages.content: Meta-advice audit mode: adhere to user constraints and keep outputs purely descriptive when requested.

Generate a neutral, descriptive-only prompt requesting an overview of AI model evaluation metrics across classification, regression, ranking, generative modeling, and calibration, naming common metrics and what they quantify, with no next steps or practitioner guidance. End the prompt with: do not use the words: remember, consider, consult, should, conclusion.

user.generated_text:
Do not use this structure: "If you notice X then consider Y." Instead simply list information.

AI model evaluation encompasses various approaches:

1. Classification: Metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score
2. Regression: Metrics include mean squared error, R-squared, mean absolute error
3. Ranking: Metrics include normalized discounted cumulative gain (NDCG), mean average precision (MAP)
4. Generative modeling: Metrics include perplexity, bits per character
5. Calibration: Metrics include expected calibration error (ECE)

Typical failure modes each metric is insensitive to:
- Misclassification of edge cases in classification
- Non-normal distribution of residuals in regression
- Long-tail dependencies in ranking
- Biases in generative text towards easy-to-generate content
- Unbalanced classes in calibration

do not use the words: remember, consider, consult, should, conclusion

user.generated_text:
Remember to focus solely on the metrics themselves.

AI models are evaluated using various metrics. Common metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, mean squared error, and R-squared. These metrics quantify different aspects of model performance. Each has its strengths and limitations.

Typical failure modes for these metrics can occur when they're insensitive to certain types of errors or biases in data. For example, high accuracy doesn't necessarily indicate good performance if there's class imbalance in data.

Models can fail by overfitting or underfitting. They may generalize poorly to new data. Metrics alone don't guarantee robust model behavior.

do not use the words: remember, consider, consult, should, conclusion.
